key_insight: An AI-assisted Lean 4 workflow can formalize a full mean-field proof of the Vlasov equation with axiom-clean verification and no gaps left as sorrys.
engineering_relevance: This shows theorem proving can be treated as a measurable human-AI engineering pipeline, with reusable mathematical components and clear interface boundaries.
method: Mathematician scopes definitions and proof decomposition.
AI agent writes the Lean 4 proof steps and fills library gaps.
Formalization is validated by compilation, no sorries, and dependency checks for axiom cleanliness and reuse.
evidence_signal: The case study reports a complete formalization of well-posedness for the nonlinear Vlasov equation, with about one-sixth of the development (49 of 299 declarations) reusable as a self-contained Mathlib layer.
watch_item: The work certifies that the Lean statements were proved as written, but whether those statements match the intended mathematics still depends on expert judgment.
paper_title: A Formalization of the Mean-Field Derivation of the Vlasov Equation: AI-Assisted Lean Formalization as a Strategy Game
